items.item: S67 made pulse data measurable, but the next internal playtest still required a human to translate rates into a script. Add an operatorNext contract that turns no-signal, stale evidence, tutorial friction, feedback gaps, and Rival Challenge conversion gaps into concrete playtest steps.
items.recipe: Extend VaultFrontPlaytestPulseSummary with operatorNext.headline, steps, and successMetric; prioritize stale evidence in action insights; cover no-signal, weak tutorial, missing feedback, weak rival action, no rival exposure, and ready-state branches in focused tests.
items.why: VaultFront's current blocker is not feature count, it is evidence quality. A playtest script generated from the same pulse truth keeps the next alpha gate executable without adding paid AI calls or another operator-only document.

items.item: The in-game KPI panel still showed pulse score and raw totals, but hid the S67 retention action rate that tells whether Rival Challenge drives one-more-match behavior. Show Rival Challenge action conversion, latest signal age, and the next operator action directly in the Playtest Pulse tile.
items.recipe: Update GameRightSidebar.renderPlaytestPulseTile to display rates.retentionAction, freshness.ageMinutes, and operatorNext.headline; add a component test proving the tile renders those signals.
items.why: This puts the launch-readiness question in the place an internal tester already opens during a match. It also keeps revenue-signal honesty intact: the UI improves observability without pretending revenue has been observed.

items.item: After shipping the S68 pulse script and KPI tile, the public-safe status files need to move past the S67 baseline. Keep PROJECT_STATUS, CURRENT_STATE, TASK_BOARD, SELF_IMPROVEMENT_LOOP, IMPLEMENT_PLAN, and closeout artifacts aligned with the new verification evidence.
items.recipe: Update public-safe truth records with the S68 shipped items, verification commands, remaining playtest/revenue flags, and closeout impact summary.
items.why: VaultFront's startup brief is machine-read from these files. Truth drift would cause the next agent to re-audit an already-shipped operator visibility gap.

skipped.reason: Still requires live browser play, but the new operatorNext script makes that playtest concrete and measurable.
Requires real checkout/supporter evidence; readiness and startup should keep warning until the signal exists.
Blocked by CANON-029 for pre-revenue free-tier cost discipline; this pass uses deterministic no-cost scripts.

executionLog.summary: Playtest pulse summaries now include operatorNext headline, steps, and successMetric with stale evidence prioritized in action insights.
The Playtest Pulse KPI tile now shows Rival Challenge action conversion, latest signal age, and the next operator action from operatorNext.
Public-safe status, current state, task board, work log, SIL, handoff, truth audit, implementation plan, and closeout audit JSON were updated for S68 evidence.
